title: audit — jurisdiction-specific retention requirements
description: Reference table of the minimum retention windows imposed by various regulatory regimes. Used by the SDUI retention-policy editor to suggest override_reason + retention_years combinations to compliance officers. NOT enforced — the tenant chooses its own retention; this is guidance.

jurisdictions.scope: Publicly-traded companies + audit firms serving them
jurisdictions.notes: Financial-mutation records. Applies to Membership, TenantSubscription, Transaction, Chargeback, Refund, Pass. Suggested override_reason: 'SOX §404 compliance — financial mutation records'.

jurisdictions.scope: Covered entities + business associates handling PHI
jurisdictions.notes: 6y minimum; 7y default aligns with SOX. Applies to planned Wave 7+ HealthNote + MedicalRecord aggregates. Suggested override_reason: 'HIPAA §164.316(b)(2)(i) — PHI-adjacent audit trail'.
Applies to any financial-record-adjacent audit trail supporting a taxable transaction. Suggested override_reason: 'IRS records-retention rule for taxable transactions'.

jurisdictions.scope: Businesses collecting personal information of California residents
jurisdictions.notes: 24-month minimum for consumer-request records (DSAR responses). Our 7y baseline exceeds. Suggested override_reason: 'CCPA §1798.185 consumer-request records'.

jurisdictions.scope: Any entity storing / processing / transmitting cardholder data
jurisdictions.notes: 1y minimum with 3 months immediately available (hot-tier). Our 90d hot + 7y baseline covers. Suggested override_reason: 'PCI-DSS Req 10.7 — audit trails for cardholder data'.

jurisdictions.scope: Any controller / processor of EU-resident personal data
jurisdictions.notes: GDPR does NOT specify a minimum retention — it specifies 'no longer than necessary'. 7 years is our balance point between Art. 30 records-of-processing (evidence for regulator inspection) and Art. 5(1)(e) minimization. Suggested override_reason: 'GDPR Art. 30 records of processing — 7y regulatory audit window'.
Applies when a tenant is registered as a PSP or processes payments under a PSP license. Suggested override_reason: 'PSD2 Art. 34 payment-transaction records'.

jurisdictions.scope: Any controller / processor of UK-resident personal data
jurisdictions.notes: Same shape as EU GDPR. Post-Brexit alignment retained. Suggested override_reason: 'UK GDPR + DPA 2018 records of processing'.

jurisdictions.scope: Any organization collecting Canadian personal information + engaging in commercial electronic messages
jurisdictions.notes: PIPEDA is principle-based; CASL requires consent-audit-trail retention. 7y default covers both. Suggested override_reason: 'PIPEDA + CASL compliance — consent + processing audit'.
Australian Privacy Principles Art. 11 requires reasonable-security-measures retention. 7y baseline covers. Suggested override_reason: 'Privacy Act 1988 APP 11 audit trail'.
SOC 2 auditors typically require 1-year audit window minimum; 7y aligned with SOX baseline. Suggested override_reason: 'SOC 2 Type II CC7.1 audit trail'.
ISO 27001 A.18.1.3 requires records-management-policy alignment with regulatory + contractual requirements. Suggested override_reason: 'ISO 27001 A.12.4.1 event logging retention'.

jurisdictions.scope: Specific investigations (SEC, FTC, DOJ, EU DPA)
jurisdictions.notes: Regulator-issued preservation orders extend retention beyond default. Suggested override_reason: 'Regulator preservation order — <regulator> case <case_id> ongoing'. override_ends_at = expected resolution date or NULL for indefinite.

Legal counsel issues a litigation-hold notice. Suggested override_reason: 'Litigation hold — <matter_name> discovery ongoing'. override_ends_at = expected resolution date or NULL until counsel releases the hold.
